commands.review_summary:
The PR moves iOS notification permission requests to Settings > Notifications, adds hosted relay disclosure and exec approval notification guidance, and updates related iOS tests.

PR surface: Other +584. Total +584 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: PR head source plus the downloaded Periphery artifact show the authorization API is unused, and the Scan iOS dead code job fails on that exact head. Not applicable as an issue reproduction because this is a PR review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer-labeled PR needs author or maintainer handling for the Periphery dead-code failure and compatibility decision before any merge path.

target.title: Consolidate iOS notification permission UX
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The iOS app had several separate paths that could request or depend on push notification authorization. That made notification permission UX easy to trigger at surprising times, and it left exec approval notification failures looking like everything was OK when notifications were disabled.

This consolidates user-managed notification authorization into Settings > Notifications and adds explicit guidance for APNs-backed approval notification flows.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Removes surprise push notification authorization prompts from bootstrap, `system.notify`, `chat.push`, and mirrored Watch fallback scheduling.
- Keeps APNs registration separate from OS notification authorization.
- Adds Settings > Notifications as the only place that presents the OS notification authorization flow.
- Adds OpenClaw hosted push relay disclosure before the OS permission request for stock hosted relay builds.
- Adds foreground exec approval guidance when an `exec.approval.requested` event arrives while notifications are unavailable.
- Adds Settings > Approvals degraded-state copy so approvals still work in-app, while clearly explaining that out-of-app approval notifications require Notifications.
- Changes the exec approval guidance suppression control from a checkbox to a third action button in the alert stack.

## User Impact

Users will manage notification permissions from one predictable Settings screen. If exec approval notifications are unavailable, the app explains the limitation and offers a route to Settings > Notifications instead of failing silently.

For stock OpenClaw-hosted relay builds, users see a disclosure before the OS notification prompt explaining that enabling Notifications sends delivery data through OpenClaw's hosted push relay.

## Evidence

- Rebased cleanly onto `origin/main` at `a182811070`.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17,OS=26.5' test -only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsSourceGuardTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/NodeAppModelInvokeTests`
  - Passed 105 tests in 2 suites.
- Local simulator E2E covered:
  - Settings > Notifications denied/not-enabled states.
  - Hosted relay disclosure and OS prompt path.
  - Exec approval notification guidance alert.
  - "Open Notifications Settings" routing.
  - "Not Now" dismiss without suppression.
  - "Don't show again" suppression as a button.
- Screenshot artifacts are preserved outside the repo in `Desktop/openclaw-apns-e2e-scratch/artifacts/`.
target.author: joshavant
